findings.title: install.ts has 7 distinct responsibilities in one 372-line module — split into composable phases
findings.evidence: (1) flag parsing, (2) prereq installer, (3) marketplace registration, (4) wrapper script generation, (5) settings.json patching, (6) plugin install loop, (7) post-install fixes (claude-hud + claude-mem + protect-mcp stub) + (8) hook smoke. Single `run()` function is 130 lines. No dependency injection — testing requires real filesystem + real `claude` binary.
findings.fix: Split into `src/install/{prereqs,marketplaces,settings,plugins,post-install,smoke}.ts`, each exporting `async function step(ctx: InstallContext): Promise<StepResult>`. `run()` becomes a 20-line orchestrator. Each step independently testable with an in-memory fs.

findings.title: checkHooksOnly and runHookSmoke are 90% identical copy-paste
findings.evidence: Both: stack-walk for hooks.json files, filter by enabledPlugins, parse JSON, iterate hooks per event, spawnSync each with fake payload, check exit + stderr for known error patterns, count failures. FAKE / FAKE_EVENTS constants are duplicated verbatim. ~50 LOC duplicated.
findings.fix: Extract `hookSmoke(opts: { silent: boolean }): { failures: HookFailure[] }` to src/lib/hook-smoke.ts. install + doctor both call it. Define FAKE_EVENTS once.

findings.title: spawnSync usage is verbose and error-handling is inconsistent — Bun.$ is shorter, safer, and the codebase already requires Bun
findings.evidence: `spawnSync('claude', ['plugin','install', spec], { encoding: 'utf8', timeout: 60_000, stdio: 'pipe' })` repeated 15+ times with slight variations. Some pass `shell: '/bin/bash'`, some don't. Some check r.status, some check r.stdout, some check r.stderr. The project's own CLAUDE.md (L18) mandates `prefer Bun.$ over raw exec in TypeScript`.
findings.fix: Replace with `await Bun.$\`claude plugin install ${spec}\`.quiet().nothrow()` — returns {stdout, stderr, exitCode}. Add `runClaude(args, opts)` helper for the `claude` binary specifically (handles required vs optional, timeout, stderr-as-warning). Cuts ~150 LOC.

findings.title: writeJsonAtomic reimplements what Bun.write + node:fs/promises.rename already give you, and the tmp file name includes pid (not unique under concurrent runs)
findings.evidence: `tmp = ${path}.tmp.${process.pid}` — two forge runs from the same pid (impossible, but) or one run with cluster fork would collide. Stripe et al. use `crypto.randomUUID()` to suffix. Also writeFile+rename is fine but Bun has `Bun.write(path, JSON.stringify(...))` which is one call.
findings.fix: `const tmp = path + '.tmp.' + crypto.randomUUID()` (or use `proper-lockfile` for a real cross-process lock if you actually care). Move atomic-write logic to one place; share across all *.json writes.

findings.title: Hand-rolled '1/3', '2/3', '✓ ...' prints duplicate spinners + progress-bar functionality from picocolors / @clack/prompts
findings.evidence: 11 occurrences of `'X/Y stepname...'` across install.ts, update.ts. Manual emoji prefix logic (`'✓'` `'✗'` `'⚠'` `'−'`). No fall-through to plain text in TTY=false. No color when piped. No progress on long-running steps (8 sudo installs run silent).
findings.fix: `@clack/prompts` (~30kb) gives `intro/outro/step/spinner/log.success/log.error` with TTY-aware fallbacks. Replaces ~40 println sites. Or stay minimal with `picocolors` (~3kb) + a 20-line `step()` helper.
